mission.title: rook: Add support for dynamically resizing volumes provisioned with Rook
mission.description: Enable dynamic PersistentVolumeClaim (PVC) expansion for Rook-Ceph provisioned volumes. Requested by 45+ users. This feature allows resizing Ceph RBD and CephFS volumes without downtime by leveraging the Kubernetes CSI volume expansion capability.

steps.title: Verify Rook-Ceph deployment and version
steps.description:
Confirm you are running Rook v1.2+ with ceph-csi, which supports volume expansion:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n rook-ceph -l app=rook-ceph-operator
kubectl get cephcluster -n rook-ceph -o jsonpath='{.items[0].status.ceph.version}'
```
Volume expansion requires Rook v1.2+ and Kubernetes v1.16+ (where PVC expansion is GA).

steps.title: Enable allowVolumeExpansion on your StorageClass
steps.description:
The key to enabling dynamic volume resizing is setting `allowVolumeExpansion: true` on your Rook-Ceph StorageClass:
```bash
kubectl get storageclass rook-ceph-block -o yaml
```
If `allowVolumeExpansion` is not set or is `false`, patch it:
```bash
kubectl patch storageclass rook-ceph-block -p '{"allowVolumeExpansion": true}'
```
For new StorageClasses, include `allowVolumeExpansion: true` in the spec.

steps.title: Resize a PersistentVolumeClaim
steps.description:
To expand an existing PVC, edit the `spec.resources.requests.storage` field:
```bash
kubectl patch pvc <pvc-name> -n <namespace> -p '{"spec": {"resources": {"requests": {"storage": "20Gi"}}}}'
```
For Ceph RBD (block storage), the resize happens online — no pod restart needed. For CephFS, the resize is also online. The CSI driver handles the underlying Ceph volume expansion automatically.

steps.title: Verify the volume was resized
steps.description:
Check that the PVC and PV reflect the new size:
```bash
kubectl get pvc <pvc-name> -n <namespace> -o jsonpath='{.status.capacity.storage}'
kubectl get pv $(kubectl get pvc <pvc-name> -n <namespace> -o jsonpath='{.spec.volumeName}') -o jsonpath='{.spec.capacity.storage}'
```
Both should show the new size. If the PVC shows `FileSystemResizePending` condition, the filesystem resize will complete when the pod using it restarts.

resolution.summary: Dynamic volume resizing for Rook-Ceph volumes is supported since Rook v1.2+ via the ceph-csi driver. Set `allowVolumeExpansion: true` on your StorageClass, then patch the PVC's `spec.resources.requests.storage` to the desired size. Ceph RBD and CephFS both support online expansion.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with Rook-Ceph v1.2+ installed. The ceph-csi driver must be used (default in Rook v1.1+).
